inline.meaning: surface names provisional (alias-law, guide-level). OUTCOME CONSTRAINT (browser-verified, demo/test): the outer display is INERT on a flex/grid ITEM — CSS blockifies a flex/grid item's outer display, so `inline`/`boxed-inline` are no-ops on a child of a flex/grid container. Enforced by lint WARNING P11 (`flow-participation-inert`, lint.ts), which reads the parent's classes from LintContext. Not a property collision, so P7 can't see it — it needs parent context.

Enforced by lint WARNING P11 (`flow-participation-inert`, lint.ts), which reads the parent's classes from LintContext. Not a property collision, so P7 can't see it — it needs parent context.

push.meaning: auto inline-start margin; consumes available inline-start free space to push the member toward inline end. Relational and socket-free, distinct from scale-backed margin.

divided.meaning: a line drawn *between* children (not on each child). `undivided` is the default.

undivided.meaning: a line drawn *between* children (not on each child). `undivided` is the default.

scroll-x.meaning: two sub-dials: scroll-x (overflow-x) and scroll-y (overflow-y) compose; scroll-auto, clip, and hidden are whole-axis (both directions), so they conflict with a per-axis dial. hidden establishes a clipping scroll container; clip forbids scrolling (R-OVERFLOW-01).

fill.meaning: whole-axis `fill` sets both inline-size and block-size, so it conflicts with a per-axis dial; `fill-inline fill-block` composes. Pure proportion (100%), no theme socket.

fill-inline.meaning: whole-axis `fill` sets both inline-size and block-size, so it conflicts with a per-axis dial; `fill-inline fill-block` composes. Pure proportion (100%), no theme socket.

fill-block.meaning: whole-axis `fill` sets both inline-size and block-size, so it conflicts with a per-axis dial; `fill-inline fill-block` composes. Pure proportion (100%), no theme socket.

base.meaning: tier-2 only; only meaningful within an isolation:isolate context. tier-2 member list is a [RULING] (values), structure frozen.

overlay.meaning: joined by OPENING; ordered at runtime by last-opened-is-topmost. choosing `modal` = promotion, not a number.

modal.meaning: joined by OPENING; ordered at runtime by last-opened-is-topmost. choosing `modal` = promotion, not a number.

popover.meaning: joined by OPENING; ordered at runtime by last-opened-is-topmost. choosing `modal` = promotion, not a number.

toast.meaning: joined by OPENING; ordered at runtime by last-opened-is-topmost. choosing `modal` = promotion, not a number.

decelerate.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

accelerate.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

standard.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

emphasized.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

symmetric.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

asymmetric.meaning: duration/delay magnitudes are open skin scales (R-MOTION-01), not grammar members.

together.meaning: stagger magnitude is an open skin scale; composes via calc(own-delay + --stagger).

sequence.meaning: stagger magnitude is an open skin scale; composes via calc(own-delay + --stagger).

cascade.meaning: stagger magnitude is an open skin scale; composes via calc(own-delay + --stagger).

active.meaning: the transient press; toggle `pressed` was folded into `selected` (Law 6b)

checked-mixed.meaning: the tri-state 'mixed/indeterminate' value as a complete word (binary arity: the word IS the value, no enum suffix).

user-invalid.meaning: platform-typical subset of invalid — :user-invalid only matches after user interaction, so it fires only where :invalid already does

out-of-range.meaning: platform-typical subset of invalid (browsers generally flag out-of-range values as :invalid too, absent novalidate)

dragging.meaning: ARIA aria-grabbed/dropeffect deprecated; backing is the live DnD event

active-descendant.meaning: inverted entailment: backing on the container

selection-subtle.meaning: conditioned-skin: a CONDITIONAL VARIANT LAYER, not a base skin axis. It writes custom properties (--selection-bg/--selection-ink), NOT background/color directly — a sink rule reads them under the `selected` condition. This is why it does NOT collide with skin-surface's background/color (same discipline as m2 longhands / motion --stagger): conditioned layers compose with base skin by writing different variables. Entails nothing itself; conditioned by `selected`/`hover`/`active`; pairs with capability `selectable`.

selection-strong.meaning: conditioned-skin: a CONDITIONAL VARIANT LAYER, not a base skin axis. It writes custom properties (--selection-bg/--selection-ink), NOT background/color directly — a sink rule reads them under the `selected` condition. This is why it does NOT collide with skin-surface's background/color (same discipline as m2 longhands / motion --stagger): conditioned layers compose with base skin by writing different variables. Entails nothing itself; conditioned by `selected`/`hover`/`active`; pairs with capability `selectable`.

patterns.meaning: open axis, two independent longhand dials grow-N (flex-grow) / shrink-N (flex-shrink) — compose one-per-dial (`grow-2 shrink-1`). The 4 corners are WHOLE-AXIS aliases writing BOTH longhands, so an alias conflicts with any other m2 word (`expandable shrink-2`, `rigid grow-2`). Per-dial default = CSS initial: an unspecified dial keeps flex-grow:0 / flex-shrink:1, so `grow-2` is NOT grow-only (it grows AND shrinks); grow-only at weight 2 is `grow-2 shrink-0`.

closed-with-parametric-member: membership is closed {content, ratio, exact}; `exact` carries a token-indexed size value (arbitrary lengths OUT in v0). Same member-level mechanism as the enumerated states — NOT open at axis scope.

closed-with-parametric-member: membership closed {span, row-span, span-all}; span/row-span carry integers, span-all is contextual (spans every column). Same member-level mechanism as m3 / enumerated states — NOT open at axis scope. Only meaningful under a grid parent.
correct only for single-line, source-ordered block/prose flow

two sub-dials: inline (padding-inline-*) and block (padding-block-*). `padding-<spacing>` is the WHOLE-AXIS form (sets both sides), so it conflicts with a per-side dial; `padding-inline-lg padding-block-sm` composes.

two sub-dials: inline (padding-inline-*) and block (padding-block-*). `padding-<spacing>` is the WHOLE-AXIS form (sets both sides), so it conflicts with a per-side dial; `padding-inline-lg padding-block-sm` composes.

two sub-dials: inline (padding-inline-*) and block (padding-block-*). `padding-<spacing>` is the WHOLE-AXIS form (sets both sides), so it conflicts with a per-side dial; `padding-inline-lg padding-block-sm` composes.

two sub-dials inline/block; `margin-<spacing>` is the whole-axis (both-sides) form. `push` owns auto inline-start margin separately because auto is relational, not scale-backed. marked-by-preference: reach for it only outside container rhythm.

two sub-dials inline/block; `margin-<spacing>` is the whole-axis (both-sides) form. `push` owns auto inline-start margin separately because auto is relational, not scale-backed. marked-by-preference: reach for it only outside container rhythm.

two sub-dials inline/block; `margin-<spacing>` is the whole-axis (both-sides) form. `push` owns auto inline-start margin separately because auto is relational, not scale-backed. marked-by-preference: reach for it only outside container rhythm.

four sub-dials, one per longhand. min-width/max-width compose as a width band; min-height/max-height compose as a height band; all four can co-occur. A future semantic check (not yet implemented) should warn when a band is inverted, e.g. min-width-lg max-width-sm.
